case.title: LUBLIN v. JAMES T. BARNES & CO.

opinion.h: LUBLIN v. JAMES T. BARNES & CO., 374 U.S. 94 (1963)
LUBLIN v. JAMES T. BARNES & CO. ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF MICHIGAN. No. 1040. Decided June 10, 1963.
opinion.p: The motion to dispense with printing the jurisdictional statement is granted.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h: W. R. ARTHUR & CO., v. WISCONSIN DEPARTMENT OF TAXATION, 374 U.S. 94 (1963)
opinion.p: 374 U.S. 94 W. R. ARTHUR & CO., INC., v. WISCONSIN DEPARTMENT OF TAXATION. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF WISCONSIN. No. 1008. Decided June 10, 1963.
Adolph J. Bieberstein, John C. Wickhem and George L. Weisbard for appellant.
George Thompson, Attorney General of Wisconsin, and Harold H. Persons and E. Weston Wood, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
